title: foeniculum vulgare var. sanguineum
intro: Foeniculum vulgare var. sanguineum, also known as Sanguine or Red Fennel, is a variant of the common fennel plant with distinctive red or purple tinted stalks. This plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its carminative and anti-inflammatory effects, and is often used to aid digestion and alleviate symptoms of menstrual cramps and menopausal symptoms. The health benefits of Foeniculum vulgare var. sanguineum are attributed to its high content of volatile oils, flavonoids, and other compounds that may help reduce pain, inflammation, and oxidative stress. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, and can also be found in some commercial digestive and menstrual relief products.
